NKJV.1: Paul and Timothy, bondservants of Jesus Christ, To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with the bishops and deacons:
NKJV.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.3: I thank my God upon every remembrance of you,
NKJV.4: always in every prayer of mine making request for you all with joy,
NKJV.5: for your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now,
NKJV.6: being confident of this very thing, that He who has begun a good work in you will complete it until the day of Jesus Christ;
NKJV.7: just as it is right for me to think this of you all, because I have you in my heart, inasmuch as both in my chains and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel, you all are partakers with me of grace.
NKJV.8: For God is my witness, how greatly I long for you all with the affection of Jesus Christ.
NKJV.9: And this I pray, that your love may abound still more and more in knowledge and all discernment,
NKJV.10: that you may approve the things that are excellent, that you may be sincere and without offense till the day of Christ,
NKJV.11: being filled with the fruits of righteousness which are by Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.
NKJV.12: But I want you to know, brethren, that the things which happened to me have actually turned out for the furtherance of the gospel,
NKJV.13: so that it has become evident to the whole palace guard, and to all the rest, that my chains are in Christ;
NKJV.14: and most of the brethren in the Lord, having become confident by my chains, are much more bold to speak the word without fear.
NKJV.15: Some indeed preach Christ even from envy and strife, and some also from goodwill:
NKJV.16: The former preach Christ from selfish ambition, not sincerely, supposing to add affliction to my chains;
NKJV.17: but the latter out of love, knowing that I am appointed for the defense of the gospel.
NKJV.18: What then? Only that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is preached; and in this I rejoice, yes, and will rejoice.
NKJV.19: For I know that this will turn out for my deliverance through your prayer and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,
NKJV.20: according to my earnest expectation and hope that in nothing I shall be ashamed, but with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death.
NKJV.21: For to me, to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
NKJV.22: But if I live on in the flesh, this will mean fruit from my labor; yet what I shall choose I cannot tell.
NKJV.23: For I am hard-pressed between the two, having a desire to depart and be with Christ, which is far better.
NKJV.24: Nevertheless to remain in the flesh is more needful for you.
NKJV.25: And being confident of this, I know that I shall remain and continue with you all for your progress and joy of faith,
NKJV.26: that your rejoicing for me may be more abundant in Jesus Christ by my coming to you again.
NKJV.27: Only let your conduct be worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that whether I come and see you or am absent, I may hear of your affairs, that you stand fast in one spirit, with one mind striving together for the faith of the gospel,
NKJV.28: and not in any way terrified by your adversaries, which is to them a proof of perdition, but to you of salvation, and that from God.
NKJV.29: For to you it has been granted on behalf of Christ, not only to believe in Him, but also to suffer for His sake,
NKJV.30: having the same conflict which you saw in me and now hear is in me.

ESV.1: Paul and Timothy, servants  of Christ Jesus, To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are at Philippi, with the overseers  and deacons:
ESV.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.3: I thank my God in all my remembrance of you,
ESV.4: always in every prayer of mine for you all making my prayer with joy,
ESV.5: because of your partnership in the gospel from the first day until now.
ESV.6: And I am sure of this, that he who began a good work in you will bring it to completion at the day of Jesus Christ.
ESV.7: It is right for me to feel this way about you all, because I hold you in my heart, for you are all partakers with me of grace,  both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel.
ESV.8: For God is my witness, how I yearn for you all with the affection of Christ Jesus.
ESV.9: And it is my prayer that your love may abound more and more, with knowledge and all discernment,
ESV.10: so that you may approve what is excellent, and so be pure and blameless for the day of Christ,
ESV.11: filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.
ESV.12: I want you to know, brothers,  that what has happened to me has really served to advance the gospel,
ESV.13: so that it has become known throughout the whole imperial guard  and to all the rest that my imprisonment is for Christ.
ESV.14: And most of the brothers, having become confident in the Lord by my imprisonment, are much more bold to speak the word  without fear.
ESV.15: Some indeed preach Christ from envy and rivalry, but others from good will.
ESV.16: The latter do it out of love, knowing that I am put here for the defense of the gospel.
ESV.17: The former proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition, not sincerely but thinking to afflict me in my imprisonment.
ESV.18: What then? Only that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is proclaimed, and in that I rejoice. To Live Is Christ Yes, and I will rejoice,
ESV.19: for I know that through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ this will turn out for my deliverance,
ESV.20: as it is my eager expectation and hope that I will not be at all ashamed, but that with full courage now as always Christ will be honored in my body, whether by life or by death.
ESV.21: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
ESV.22: If I am to live in the flesh, that means fruitful labor for me. Yet which I shall choose I cannot tell.
ESV.23: I am hard pressed between the two. My desire is to depart and be with Christ, for that is far better.
ESV.24: But to remain in the flesh is more necessary on your account.
ESV.25: Convinced of this, I know that I will remain and continue with you all, for your progress and joy in the faith,
ESV.26: so that in me you may have ample cause to glory in Christ Jesus, because of my coming to you again.
ESV.27: Only let your manner of life be worthy  of the gospel of Christ, so that whether I come and see you or am absent, I may hear of you that you are standing firm in one spirit, with one mind striving side by side for the faith of the gospel,
ESV.28: and not frightened in anything by your opponents. This is a clear sign to them of their destruction, but of your salvation, and that from God.
ESV.29: For it has been granted to you that for the sake of Christ you should not only believe in him but also suffer for his sake,
ESV.30: engaged in the same conflict that you saw I had and now hear that I still have.

NASB.1: Paul and Timothy, bond-servants of Christ Jesus, To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, including the overseers and deacons:
NASB.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.3: I thank my God in all my remembrance of you,
NASB.4: always offering prayer with joy in my every prayer for you all,
NASB.5: in view of your participation in the gospel from the first day until now.
NASB.6: [For I am] confident of this very thing, that He who began a good work in you will perfect it until the day of Christ Jesus.
NASB.7: For it is only right for me to feel this way about you all, because I have you in my heart, since both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel, you all are partakers of grace with me.
NASB.8: For God is my witness, how I long for you all with the affection of Christ Jesus.
NASB.9: And this I pray, that your love may abound still more and more in real knowledge and all discernment,
NASB.10: so that you may approve the things that are excellent, in order to be sincere and blameless until the day of Christ;
NASB.11: having been filled with the fruit of righteousness which [comes] through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.
NASB.12: Now I want you to know, brethren, that my circumstances have turned out for the greater progress of the gospel,
NASB.13: so that my imprisonment in [the cause of] Christ has become well known throughout the whole praetorian guard and to everyone else,
NASB.14: and that most of the brethren, trusting in the Lord because of my imprisonment, have far more courage to speak the word of God without fear.
NASB.15: Some, to be sure, are preaching Christ even from envy and strife, but some also from good will;
NASB.16: the latter [do it] out of love, knowing that I am appointed for the defense of the gospel;
NASB.17: the former proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition rather than from pure motives, thinking to cause me distress in my imprisonment.
NASB.18: What then? Only that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is proclaimed; and in this I rejoice. Yes, and I will rejoice,
NASB.19: for I know that this will turn out for my deliverance through your prayers and the provision of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,
NASB.20: according to my earnest expectation and hope, that I will not be put to shame in anything, but [that] with all boldness, Christ will even now, as always, be exalted in my body, whether by life or by death.
NASB.21: For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.
NASB.22: But if [I am] to live [on] in the flesh, this [will mean] fruitful labor for me; and I do not know which to choose.
NASB.23: But I am hard-pressed from both [directions], having the desire to depart and be with Christ, for [that] is very much better;
NASB.24: yet to remain on in the flesh is more necessary for your sake.
NASB.25: Convinced of this, I know that I will remain and continue with you all for your progress and joy in the faith,
NASB.26: so that your proud confidence in me may abound in Christ Jesus through my coming to you again.
NASB.27: Only conduct yourselves in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that whether I come and see you or remain absent, I will hear of you that you are standing firm in one spirit, with one mind striving together for the faith of the gospel;
NASB.28: in no way alarmed by [your] opponents-- which is a sign of destruction for them, but of salvation for you, and that [too], from God.
NASB.29: For to you it has been granted for Christ's sake, not only to believe in Him, but also to suffer for His sake,
NASB.30: experiencing the same conflict which you saw in me, and now hear [to be] in me.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul and Timothy, slaves of Christ Jesus. I am writing to all of God’s holy people in Philippi who belong to Christ Jesus, including the church leaders and deacons.
NLT.2: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
NLT.3: Every time I think of you, I give thanks to my God.
NLT.4: Whenever I pray, I make my requests for all of you with joy,
NLT.5: for you have been my partners in spreading the Good News about Christ from the time you first heard it until now.
NLT.6: And I am certain that God, who began the good work within you, will continue his work until it is finally finished on the day when Christ Jesus returns.
NLT.7: So it is right that I should feel as I do about all of you, for you have a special place in my heart. You share with me the special favor of God, both in my imprisonment and in defending and confirming the truth of the Good News.
NLT.8: God knows how much I love you and long for you with the tender compassion of Christ Jesus.
NLT.9: I pray that your love will overflow more and more, and that you will keep on growing in knowledge and understanding.
NLT.10: For I want you to understand what really matters, so that you may live pure and blameless lives until the day of Christ’s return.
NLT.11: May you always be filled with the fruit of your salvation — the righteous character produced in your life by Jesus Christ — for this will bring much glory and praise to God.
NLT.12: And I want you to know, my dear brothers and sisters, that everything that has happened to me here has helped to spread the Good News.
NLT.13: For everyone here, including the whole palace guard, knows that I am in chains because of Christ.
NLT.14: And because of my imprisonment, most of the believers here have gained confidence and boldly speak God’s message without fear.
NLT.15: It’s true that some are preaching out of jealousy and rivalry. But others preach about Christ with pure motives.
NLT.16: They preach because they love me, for they know I have been appointed to defend the Good News.
NLT.17: Those others do not have pure motives as they preach about Christ. They preach with selfish ambition, not sincerely, intending to make my chains more painful to me.
NLT.18: But that doesn’t matter. Whether their motives are false or genuine, the message about Christ is being preached either way, so I rejoice. And I will continue to rejoice.
NLT.19: For I know that as you pray for me and the Spirit of Jesus Christ helps me, this will lead to my deliverance.
NLT.20: For I fully expect and hope that I will never be ashamed, but that I will continue to be bold for Christ, as I have been in the past. And I trust that my life will bring honor to Christ, whether I live or die.
NLT.21: For to me, living means living for Christ, and dying is even better.
NLT.22: But if I live, I can do more fruitful work for Christ. So I really don’t know which is better.
NLT.23: I’m torn between two desires: I long to go and be with Christ, which would be far better for me.
NLT.24: But for your sakes, it is better that I continue to live.
NLT.25: Knowing this, I am convinced that I will remain alive so I can continue to help all of you grow and experience the joy of your faith.
NLT.26: And when I come to you again, you will have even more reason to take pride in Christ Jesus because of what he is doing through me.
NLT.27: Above all, you must live as citizens of heaven, conducting yourselves in a manner worthy of the Good News about Christ. Then, whether I come and see you again or only hear about you, I will know that you are standing together with one spirit and one purpose, fighting together for the faith, which is the Good News.
NLT.28: Don’t be intimidated in any way by your enemies. This will be a sign to them that they are going to be destroyed, but that you are going to be saved, even by God himself.
NLT.29: For you have been given not only the privilege of trusting in Christ but also the privilege of suffering for him.
NLT.30: We are in this struggle together. You have seen my struggle in the past, and you know that I am still in the midst of it.

WEB.1: Paul and Timothy, servants of Jesus Christ; To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are at Philippi, with the overseers  and servants :
WEB.2: Grace to you, and peace from God, our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.3: I thank my God whenever I remember you,
WEB.4: always in every request of mine on behalf of you all making my requests with joy,
WEB.5: for your partnership  in furtherance of the Good News from the first day until now;
WEB.6: being confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will complete it until the day of Jesus Christ.
WEB.7: It is even right for me to think this way on behalf of all of you, because I have you in my heart, because, both in my bonds and in the defense and confirmation of the Good News, you all are partakers with me of grace.
WEB.8: For God is my witness, how I long after all of you in the tender mercies of Christ Jesus.
WEB.9: This I pray, that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and all discernment;
WEB.10: so that you may approve the things that are excellent; that you may be sincere and without offense to the day of Christ;
WEB.11: being filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.
WEB.12: Now I desire to have you know, brothers,  that the things which happened to me have turned out rather to the progress of the Good News;
WEB.13: so that it became evident to the whole palace  guard, and to all the rest, that my bonds are in Christ;
WEB.14: and that most of the brothers in the Lord, being confident through my bonds, are more abundantly bold to speak the word of God without fear.
WEB.15: Some indeed preach Christ even out of envy and strife, and some also out of good will.
WEB.16: The former insincerely preach Christ from selfish ambition, thinking that they add affliction to my chains;
WEB.17: but the latter out of love, knowing that I am appointed for the defense of the Good News.
WEB.18: What does it matter? Only that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is proclaimed. I rejoice in this, yes, and will rejoice.
WEB.19: For I know that this will turn out to my salvation, through your supplication and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,
WEB.20: according to my earnest expectation and hope, that I will in no way be disappointed, but with all boldness, as always, now also Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life, or by death.
WEB.21: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
WEB.22: But if I live on in the flesh, this will bring fruit from my work; yet I don’t know what I will choose.
WEB.23: But I am in a dilemma between the two, having the desire to depart and be with Christ, which is far better.
WEB.24: Yet, to remain in the flesh is more needful for your sake.
WEB.25: Having this confidence, I know that I will remain, yes, and remain with you all, for your progress and joy in the faith,
WEB.26: that your rejoicing may abound in Christ Jesus in me through my presence with you again.
WEB.27: Only let your way of life be worthy of the Good News of Christ, that, whether I come and see you or am absent, I may hear of your state, that you stand firm in one spirit, with one soul striving for the faith of the Good News;
WEB.28: and in nothing frightened by the adversaries, which is for them a proof of destruction, but to you of salvation, and that from God.
WEB.29: Because it has been granted to you on behalf of Christ, not only to believe in him, but also to suffer on his behalf,
WEB.30: having the same conflict which you saw in me, and now hear is in me.

CSB17.1: Paul <sup>ⓖ</sup> and Timothy, <sup>ⓗ</sup> servants of Christ Jesus: To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, <sup>ⓘ</sup> including the overseers <sup>ⓙ</sup> and deacons. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.3: I give thanks to my God for every remembrance of you, <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.4: always praying with joy for all of you in my every prayer,
CSB17.5: because of your partnership in the gospel from the first day <sup>ⓛ</sup> until now. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.6: I am sure of this, that he who started a good work <sup>ⓝ</sup> in you <sup>[2]</sup> will carry it on to completion <sup>ⓐ</sup> until the day of Christ Jesus.
CSB17.7: Indeed, it is right <sup>ⓑ</sup> for me to think this way about all of you, because I have you in my heart, <sup>[3]</sup> and you are all partners with me in grace, both in my imprisonment <sup>ⓒ</sup> and in the defense <sup>ⓓ</sup> and confirmation of the gospel.
CSB17.8: For God is my witness, <sup>ⓔ</sup> how deeply I miss all of you <sup>ⓕ</sup> with the affection of Christ Jesus.
CSB17.9: And I pray this: that your love <sup>ⓖ</sup> will keep on growing <sup>ⓗ</sup> in knowledge and every kind of discernment, <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.10: so that you may approve the things that are superior <sup>ⓙ</sup> and may be pure <sup>ⓚ</sup> and blameless <sup>ⓛ</sup> in the day of Christ, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.11: filled with the fruit <sup>ⓝ</sup> of righteousness <sup>ⓐ</sup> that comes through Jesus Christ to the glory <sup>ⓑ</sup> and praise of God.
CSB17.12: Now I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that what has happened to me has actually advanced the gospel, <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.13: so that it has become known throughout the whole imperial guard, and to everyone else, that my imprisonment is because I am in Christ.
CSB17.14: Most of the brothers have gained confidence in the Lord from my imprisonment and dare even more to speak the word <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup> fearlessly.
CSB17.15: To be sure, some preach Christ out of envy and rivalry, <sup>ⓔ</sup> but others out of good will.
CSB17.16: These preach out of love, <sup>ⓕ</sup> knowing that I am appointed for the defense of the gospel;
CSB17.17: the others proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition, <sup>ⓖ</sup> not sincerely, thinking that they will cause me trouble in my imprisonment.
CSB17.18: What does it matter? Only that in every way, whether from false motives <sup>ⓗ</sup> or true, Christ is proclaimed, and in this I rejoice. Yes, and I will continue to rejoice
CSB17.19: because I know this will lead to my salvation <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> through your prayers and help from the Spirit <sup>ⓙ</sup> of Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.20: My eager expectation and hope <sup>ⓛ</sup> is that I will not be ashamed about anything, but that now as always, with all courage, Christ will be highly honored in my body, whether by life <sup>ⓜ</sup> or by death. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.21: For me, to live is Christ <sup>ⓐ</sup> and to die is gain. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.22: Now if I live on in the flesh, this means fruitful <sup>ⓒ</sup> work <sup>ⓓ</sup> for me; and I don’t know which one I should choose.
CSB17.23: I am torn between the two. I long to depart and be with Christ <sup>ⓔ</sup>  — which is far better <sup>ⓕ</sup>  —
CSB17.24: but to remain in the flesh is more necessary for your sake.
CSB17.25: Since I am persuaded of this, I know that I will remain and continue with all of you for your progress and joy in the faith, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.26: so that, because of my coming to you again, your boasting <sup>ⓗ</sup> in Christ Jesus <sup>ⓘ</sup> may abound.
CSB17.27: Just one thing: As citizens of heaven, live your life <sup>ⓙ</sup> worthy of the gospel of Christ. Then, whether I come and see you or am absent, I will hear about you that you are standing firm <sup>ⓚ</sup> in one <sup>ⓛ</sup> spirit, in one accord, <sup>[6]</sup> contending together for the faith <sup>ⓜ</sup> of the gospel,
CSB17.28: not being frightened in any way by your opponents. <sup>ⓝ</sup> This is a sign of destruction for them, <sup>ⓐ</sup> but of your salvation <sup>ⓑ</sup>  — and this is from God.
CSB17.29: For it has been granted to you on Christ’s behalf not only to believe <sup>ⓒ</sup> in him, but also to suffer for him,
CSB17.30: since you are engaged in the same struggle that you saw I had and now hear that I have.

AMP.1:
Paul and Timothy, bond-servants of Christ Jesus (the Messiah, the Anointed),
To all the saints (God's people) in Christ Jesus who are at Philippi, including the overseers and deacons:
AMP.2: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.3: I thank my God in every remembrance of you,
AMP.4: always offering every prayer of mine with joy [and with specific requests] for all of you,
AMP.5: [thanking God] for your participation and partnership [both your comforting fellowship and gracious contributions] in [advancing] the good news [regarding salvation] from the first day [you heard it] until now.
AMP.6: I am convinced and confident of this very thing, that He who has begun a good work in you will [continue to] perfect and complete it until the day of Christ Jesus [the time of His return].
AMP.7: It is right for me to feel this way about you, because [you have me in your heart as] I have you in my heart, since both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the good news [regarding salvation], all of you share in [His matchless] grace with me.
AMP.8: For God is my witness, how I long for all of you with the affection of Christ Jesus [whose great love fills me].
AMP.9: And this I pray, that your love may abound more and more [displaying itself in greater depth] in real knowledge and in practical insight,
AMP.10: so that you may learn to recognize and treasure what is excellent [identifying the best, and distinguishing moral differences], and that you may be pure and blameless until the day of Christ [actually living lives that lead others away from sin];
AMP.11: filled with the fruit of righteousness which comes through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God [so that His glory may be both revealed and recognized].
AMP.12: Now I want you to know, believers, that what has happened to me [this imprisonment that was meant to stop me] has actually served to advance [the spread of] the good news [regarding salvation].
AMP.13: My imprisonment in [the cause of] Christ has become common knowledge throughout the whole praetorian (imperial) guard and to everyone else.
AMP.14: Because of my chains [seeing that I am doing well and that God is accomplishing great things], most of the brothers have renewed confidence in the Lord, and have far more courage to speak the word of God [concerning salvation] without fear [of the consequences, seeing that God can work His good in all circumstances].
AMP.15: Some, it is true, are [actually] preaching Christ out of envy and rivalry [toward me—for no better reason than a competitive spirit or misguided ambition], but others out of goodwill and a loyal spirit [toward me].
AMP.16: The latter [preach Christ] out of love, because they know that I have been put here [by God on purpose] for the defense of the gospel;
AMP.17: but the former preach Christ [insincerely] out of selfish ambition [just self-promotion], thinking that they are causing me distress in my imprisonment.
AMP.18:
What then [does it matter]? So long as in every way, whether in pretense [for self-promotion] or in all honesty [to spread the truth], Christ is being preached; and in this I rejoice.
Yes, and I will rejoice [later as well],
AMP.19: for I know [with confidence] that this will turn out for my deliverance and spiritual well-being, through your prayers and the [superabundant] supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ [which upholds me].
AMP.20: It is my own eager expectation and hope, that [looking toward the future] I will not disgrace myself nor be ashamed in anything, but that with courage and the utmost freedom of speech, even now as always, Christ will be magnified and exalted in my body, whether by life or by death.
AMP.21: For to me, to live is Christ [He is my source of joy, my reason to live] and to die is gain [for I will be with Him in eternity].
AMP.22: If, however, it is to be life here and I am to go on living, this will mean useful and productive service for me; so I do not know which to choose [if I am given that choice].
AMP.23: But I am hard-pressed between the two. I have the desire to leave [this world] and be with Christ, for that is far, far better;
AMP.24: yet to remain in my body is more necessary and essential for your sake.
AMP.25: Since I am convinced of this, I know that I will remain and continue with all of you for your progress and joy in the faith,
AMP.26: so that your rejoicing for me may overflow in Christ Jesus through my coming to you again.
AMP.27: Only [be sure to] lead your lives in a manner [that will be] worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that whether I do come and see you or remain absent, I will hear about you that you are standing firm in one spirit [and one purpose], with one mind striving side by side [as if in combat] for the faith of the gospel.
AMP.28: And in no way be alarmed or intimidated [in anything] by your opponents, for such [constancy and fearlessness on your part] is a [clear] sign [a proof and a seal] for them of [their impending] destruction, but [a clear sign] for you of deliverance and salvation, and that too, from God.
AMP.29: For you have been granted [the privilege] for Christ's sake, not only to believe and confidently trust in Him, but also to suffer for His sake,
AMP.30: [and so you are] experiencing the same [kind of] conflict which you saw me endure, and which you hear to be mine now.

MSG.1: Paul and Timothy, both of us committed servants of Christ Jesus, write this letter to all the Christians in Philippi, pastors and ministers included.
MSG.2: We greet you with the grace and peace that comes from God our Father and our Master, Jesus Christ.
MSG.3: Every time you cross my mind, I break out in exclamations of thanks to God.
MSG.4: Each exclamation is a trigger to prayer. I find myself praying for you with a glad heart.
MSG.5: I am so pleased that you have continued on in this with us, believing and proclaiming God's Message, from the day you heard it right up to the present.
MSG.6: There has never been the slightest doubt in my mind that the God who started this great work in you would keep at it and bring it to a flourishing finish on the very day Christ Jesus appears.
MSG.7: It's not at all fanciful for me to think this way about you. My prayers and hopes have deep roots in reality. You have, after all, stuck with me all the way from the time I was thrown in jail, put on trial, and came out of it in one piece. All along you have experienced with me the most generous help from God.
MSG.8: He knows how much I love and miss you these days. Sometimes I think I feel as strongly about you as Christ does!
MSG.9: So this is my prayer: that your love will flourish and that you will not only love much but well. Learn to love appropriately. You need to use your head and test your feelings
MSG.10: so that your love is sincere and intelligent, not sentimental gush. Live a lover's life, circumspect and exemplary, a life Jesus will be proud of:
MSG.11: bountiful in fruits from the soul, making Jesus Christ attractive to all, getting everyone involved in the glory and praise of God.
MSG.12: I want to report to you, friends, that my imprisonment here has had the opposite of its intended effect. Instead of being squelched, the Message has actually prospered.
MSG.13: All the soldiers here, and everyone else too, found out that I'm in jail because of this Messiah. That piqued their curiosity, and now they've learned all about him.
MSG.14: Not only that, but most of the Christians here have become far more sure of themselves in the faith than ever, speaking out fearlessly about God, about the Messiah.
MSG.15: It's true that some here preach Christ because with me out of the way, they think they'll step right into the spotlight. But the others do it with the best heart in the world.
MSG.16: One group is motivated by pure love, knowing that I am here defending the Message, wanting to help.
MSG.17: The others, now that I'm out of the picture, are merely greedy, hoping to get something out of it for themselves. Their motives are bad. They see me as their competition, and so the worse it goes for me, the better—they think—for them.
MSG.18: So how am I to respond? I've decided that I really don't care about their motives, whether mixed, bad, or indifferent. Every time one of them opens his mouth, Christ is proclaimed, so I just cheer them on! And I'm going to keep that celebration going
MSG.19: because I know how it's going to turn out. Through your faithful prayers and the generous response of the Spirit of Jesus Christ, everything he wants to do in and through me will be done.
MSG.20: I can hardly wait to continue on my course. I don't expect to be embarrassed in the least. On the contrary, everything happening to me in this jail only serves to make Christ more accurately known, regardless of whether I live or die. They didn't shut me up; they gave me a pulpit!
MSG.21: Alive, I'm Christ's messenger; dead, I'm his bounty. Life versus even more life! I can't lose.
MSG.22: As long as I'm alive in this body, there is good work for me to do. If I had to choose right now, I hardly know which I'd choose.
MSG.23: Hard choice! The desire to break camp here and be with Christ is powerful. Some days I can think of nothing better
MSG.24: . But most days, because of what you are going through, I am sure that it's better for me to stick it out here.
MSG.25: So I plan to be around awhile, companion to you as your growth and joy in this life of trusting God continues.
MSG.26: You can start looking forward to a great reunion when I come visit you again. We'll be praising Christ, enjoying each other.
MSG.27: Meanwhile, live in such a way that you are a credit to the Message of Christ. Let nothing in your conduct hang on whether I come or not. Your conduct must be the same whether I show up to see things for myself or hear of it from a distance. Stand united, singular in vision, contending for people's trust in the Message, the good news,
MSG.28: not flinching or dodging in the slightest before the opposition. Your courage and unity will show them what they're up against: defeat for them, victory for you—and both because of God.
MSG.29: There's far more to this life than trusting in Christ. There's also suffering for him. And the suffering is as much a gift as the trusting.
MSG.30: You're involved in the same kind of struggle you saw me go through, on which you are now getting an updated report in this letter.

NIV.1:
Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus,
To all the saints in Christ Jesus at Philippi, together with the overseers and deacons:
NIV.2: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.3:
Thanksgiving and Prayer
I thank my God every time I remember you.
NIV.4: In all my prayers for all of you, I always pray with joy
NIV.5: because of your partnership in the gospel from the first day until now,
NIV.6: being confident of this, that he who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus.
NIV.7: It is right for me to feel this way about all of you, since I have you in my heart; for whether I am in chains or defending and confirming the gospel, all of you share in God’s grace with me.
NIV.8: God can testify how I long for all of you with the affection of Christ Jesus.
NIV.9: And this is my prayer: that your love may abound more and more in knowledge and depth of insight,
NIV.10: so that you may be able to discern what is best and may be pure and blameless until the day of Christ,
NIV.11: filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ—to the glory and praise of God.
NIV.12:
Paul’s Chains Advance the Gospel
Now I want you to know, brothers, that what has happened to me has really served to advance the gospel.
NIV.13: As a result, it has become clear throughout the whole palace guard and to everyone else that I am in chains for Christ.
NIV.14: Because of my chains, most of the brothers in the Lord have been encouraged to speak the word of God more courageously and fearlessly.
NIV.15: It is true that some preach Christ out of envy and rivalry, but others out of goodwill.
NIV.16: The latter do so in love, knowing that I am put here for the defense of the gospel.
NIV.17: The former preach Christ out of selfish ambition, not sincerely, supposing that they can stir up trouble for me while I am in chains.
NIV.18:
But what does it matter? The important thing is that in every way, whether from false motives or true, Christ is preached. And because of this I rejoice.
Yes, and I will continue to rejoice,
NIV.19: for I know that through your prayers and the help given by the Spirit of Jesus Christ, what has happened to me will turn out for my deliverance.
NIV.20: I eagerly expect and hope that I will in no way be ashamed, but will have sufficient courage so that now as always Christ will be exalted in my body, whether by life or by death.
NIV.21: For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.
NIV.22: If I am to go on living in the body, this will mean fruitful labor for me. Yet what shall I choose? I do not know!
NIV.23: I am torn between the two: I desire to depart and be with Christ, which is better by far;
NIV.24: but it is more necessary for you that I remain in the body.
NIV.25: Convinced of this, I know that I will remain, and I will continue with all of you for your progress and joy in the faith,
NIV.26: so that through my being with you again your joy in Christ Jesus will overflow on account of me.
NIV.27: Whatever happens, conduct yourselves in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ. Then, whether I come and see you or only hear about you in my absence, I will know that you stand firm in one spirit, contending as one man for the faith of the gospel
NIV.28: without being frightened in any way by those who oppose you. This is a sign to them that they will be destroyed, but that you will be saved—and that by God.
NIV.29: For it has been granted to you on behalf of Christ not only to believe on him, but also to suffer for him,
NIV.30: since you are going through the same struggle you saw I had, and now hear that I still have.

NRSVCE.1: Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus, To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with the bishops and deacons:
NRSVCE.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.3: I thank my God every time I remember you,
NRSVCE.4: constantly praying with joy in every one of my prayers for all of you,
NRSVCE.5: because of your sharing in the gospel from the first day until now.
NRSVCE.6: I am confident of this, that the one who began a good work among you will bring it to completion by the day of Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.7: It is right for me to think this way about all of you, because you hold me in your heart, for all of you share in God’s grace with me, both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel.
NRSVCE.8: For God is my witness, how I long for all of you with the compassion of Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.9: And this is my prayer, that your love may overflow more and more with knowledge and full insight
NRSVCE.10: to help you to determine what is best, so that in the day of Christ you may be pure and blameless,
NRSVCE.11: having produced the harvest of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ for the glory and praise of God.
NRSVCE.12: I want you to know, beloved, that what has happened to me has actually helped to spread the gospel,
NRSVCE.13: so that it has become known throughout the whole imperial guard and to everyone else that my imprisonment is for Christ;
NRSVCE.14: and most of the brothers and sisters, having been made confident in the Lord by my imprisonment, dare to speak the word with greater boldness and without fear.
NRSVCE.15: Some proclaim Christ from envy and rivalry, but others from goodwill.
NRSVCE.16: These proclaim Christ out of love, knowing that I have been put here for the defense of the gospel;
NRSVCE.17: the others proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition, not sincerely but intending to increase my suffering in my imprisonment.
NRSVCE.18: What does it matter? Just this, that Christ is proclaimed in every way, whether out of false motives or true; and in that I rejoice. Yes, and I will continue to rejoice,
NRSVCE.19: for I know that through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ this will turn out for my deliverance.
NRSVCE.20: It is my eager expectation and hope that I will not be put to shame in any way, but that by my speaking with all boldness, Christ will be exalted now as always in my body, whether by life or by death.
NRSVCE.21: For to me, living is Christ and dying is gain.
NRSVCE.22: If I am to live in the flesh, that means fruitful labor for me; and I do not know which I prefer.
NRSVCE.23: I am hard pressed between the two: my desire is to depart and be with Christ, for that is far better;
NRSVCE.24: but to remain in the flesh is more necessary for you.
NRSVCE.25: Since I am convinced of this, I know that I will remain and continue with all of you for your progress and joy in faith,
NRSVCE.26: so that I may share abundantly in your boasting in Christ Jesus when I come to you again.
NRSVCE.27: Only, live your life in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that, whether I come and see you or am absent and hear about you, I will know that you are standing firm in one spirit, striving side by side with one mind for the faith of the gospel,
NRSVCE.28: and are in no way intimidated by your opponents. For them this is evidence of their destruction, but of your salvation. And this is God’s doing.
NRSVCE.29: For he has graciously granted you the privilege not only of believing in Christ, but of suffering for him as well —
NRSVCE.30: since you are having the same struggle that you saw I had and now hear that I still have.

NET.1: From Paul and Timothy, slaves of Christ Jesus, to all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with the overseers and deacons.
NET.2: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
NET.3: I thank my God every time I remember you.
NET.4: I always pray with joy in my every prayer for all of you
NET.5: because of your participation in the gospel from the first day until now.
NET.6: For I am sure of this very thing, that the one who began a good work in you will perfect it until the day of Christ Jesus.
NET.7: For it is right for me to think this about all of you, because I have you in my heart, since both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel all of you became partners in God’s grace together with me.
NET.8: For God is my witness that I long for all of you with the affection of Christ Jesus.
NET.9: And I pray this, that your love may abound even more and more in knowledge and every kind of insight
NET.10: so that you can decide what is best, and thus be sincere and blameless for the day of Christ,
NET.11: filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ to the glory and praise of God.
NET.12: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that my situation has actually turned out to advance the gospel:
NET.13: The whole imperial guard and everyone else knows that I am in prison for the sake of Christ,
NET.14: and most of the brothers and sisters, having confidence in the Lord because of my imprisonment, now more than ever dare to speak the word fearlessly.
NET.15: Some, to be sure, are preaching Christ from envy and rivalry, but others from goodwill.
NET.16: The latter do so from love because they know that I am placed here for the defense of the gospel.
NET.17: The former proclaim Christ from selfish ambition, not sincerely, because they think they can cause trouble for me in my imprisonment.
NET.18: What is the result? Only that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is being proclaimed, and in this I rejoice. Yes, and I will continue to rejoice,
NET.19: for I know that this will turn out for my deliverance through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ.
NET.20: My confident hope is that I will in no way be ashamed but that with complete boldness, even now as always, Christ will be exalted in my body, whether I live or die.
NET.21: For to me, living is Christ and dying is gain.
NET.22: Now if I am to go on living in the body, this will mean productive work for me, yet I don’t know which I prefer:
NET.23: I feel torn between the two, because I have a desire to depart and be with Christ, which is better by far,
NET.24: but it is more vital for your sake that I remain in the body.
NET.25: And since I am sure of this, I know that I will remain and continue with all of you for the sake of your progress and joy in the faith,
NET.26: so that what you can be proud of may increase because of me in Christ Jesus, when I come back to you.
NET.27: Only conduct yourselves in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ so that — whether I come and see you or whether I remain absent — I should hear that you are standing firm in one spirit, with one mind, by contending side by side for the faith of the gospel,
NET.28: and by not being intimidated in any way by your opponents. This is a sign of their destruction, but of your salvation — a sign which is from God.
NET.29: For it has been granted to you not only to believe in Christ but also to suffer for him,
NET.30: since you are encountering the same conflict that you saw me face and now hear that I am facing.
